senses.definition: A breed of domestic chicken with a distinctive ornamental plumage and feathered feet, originating from the Netherlands.
examples: The bobbejaans strutted proudly in their coop.

senses.definition: A device that floats on water to mark a position or to indicate a bite on a fishing line; also a device on a fishing reel that releases line.
examples: The bobber floated on the lake.
He adjusted the bobber to cast farther.

senses.definition: To bounce lightly or move up and down.
examples: The rope bobbered in the breeze.

senses.definition: Places or activities where bobbers (fishing floats) are used; metaphorically, situations that are lively or noisy in a playful way.
examples: The bobberies along the river drew many anglers.
Her stories bring bobberies to our campfire.

senses.definition: Multiple devices used to float fishing lines, or figuratively, anything that floats or marks a boundary.
examples: He attached two bobbers to the line.
The water surface had several bobbers on it.

senses.definition: A noisy disturbance or commotion; a lively disturbance or uproar.
examples: The street party turned into a bobbery at night.
There was a bobbery outside the stadium after the game.

senses.definition: A woman's name or a nickname; or a small bone or small object in some dialects; a form of nurse or baby in old usage.
examples: We met a nurse named Bobbie.
Her nickname was Bobbie.

senses.definition: A plural form of Bobbie; can refer to police officers in British slang (from
examples: The bobbies kept the crowd in order.

senses.definition: A spool or cylinder around which thread is wound, used in sewing and textile work.
examples: Fill the bobbin with cotton thread.
The machine wound the bobbin automatically.

senses.definition: A person who winds or designs bobbins; may refer to a tool or craftsperson in textile work.
examples: The bobbiner prepared many bobbins for the loom.

senses.definition: A lightweight, open-weave fabric used for nets and dressmaking; often used in millinery and fashion.
examples: The veil was made of bobbinet fabric.

senses.definition: Plural of bobbinet; multiple pieces of bobbinet fabric.
examples: A collection of bobbinets were displayed.

senses.definition: The act of moving up and down or bouncing, often on water or while floating.
examples: The boat was in bobbing motion on the waves.

senses.definition: Present participle of bob; to move up and down or float.
examples: The cork was bobbing in the stream.

senses.definition: A hypothetical or fictional mineral or material named after bobbins; not a standard English term.
examples: The artifact was described as bobbinite in the manuscript.

senses.definition: Multiple spools used for winding thread or yarn.
examples: A box of spare bobbins sits on the shelf.

senses.definition: A decorative sewing technique where thread is stitched to create patterning, often on fabric edges.
examples: She practiced bobbinwork for decorative borders.

senses.definition: Characteristic of someone who speaks with a heavy or comical bobby sense; slightly silly or odd in speech.
examples: His bobbish accent made the room laugh.

senses.definition: In a bobbish manner; in a playful, silly, or mock-serious way.
examples: She spoke bobbishly, as if telling a joke.

senses.definition: A surname or a place name; also a verb meaning to cut away with a bobbit knife (obsolete).
examples: The bobbitt family lived nearby.

senses.definition: Past tense of bobbit; to cut with a bobbit knife or to remove by cutting.
examples: The fabric was bobbitted to remove the loose thread.

senses.definition: The act of cutting with a bobbit knife or, in a textile context, trimming edges.
examples: Bobbitting the seam took careful hands.

senses.definition: Plural of Bobbitt; may refer to people with the surname or historical references.
examples: The BOBBITTS family attended the reunion.

senses.definition: A small bump or swelling; a toy that shakes and bobs; also a fake or mistaken impression (bobble) in sports.
examples: The bobble on the jacket was noticeable.
Take care of the bobble on the hat.

senses.definition: To move or cause to move with a light rise and fall; to bob up and down.
examples: The key bobbled in the pocket.
The head bobbles as she nods.

senses.definition: Past tense of bobble; moved with a light up-and-down motion or became twisted with a knot in the fabric.
examples: The thread bobbled under pressure.
Her hair bobbled in the wind.

senses.definition: A small figurine with an oversized head that nods back and forth; often used as a novelty item or trophy.
examples: A bobblehead of the coach sat on the shelf.

senses.definition: Multiple bobblehead figurines; plural form.
examples: They collected several bobbleheads of players.
